title: QualityReport
description: Commit- and environment-stamped aggregate of repository quality signals. This schema deliberately separates diagnostic signals from required gates and never collapses them into a single vanity score (issue #6213). Each signal family carries its own availability, source, role, and decision use. When a signal cannot be computed from an existing authoritative surface, it is recorded as 'unavailable' or 'deferred' with a 'source_gap'; metrics are never invented and gates are never silently changed.

report_id.description: Stable identifier for this report instance, typically commit + generated_at.

generated_at.description: UTC timestamp the report was produced.

scope.description: What the report covers: package(s), changed-files view, and the baseline it is compared against.

cadence.description: When the report is produced and which lane owns it.

provenance.description: Commit and environment provenance required for reproducibility.

tree_clean.description: True only when the report was produced from a clean working tree (git dirty == false). A false value is a reproducibility caveat, not a failure by itself.

inputs.description: Declared reproducible command and the authoritative source surfaces read to build the report.

notes.description: Optional free-form caveats (e.g. degraded/fallback execution notes). Not a substitute for structured signals.

role.description: Whether the signal is a required gate that can block merge, or a diagnostic that informs judgment without blocking. This distinction is preserved throughout the report and must never be collapsed.

availability.description: 'available' = computed from an existing authoritative surface; 'unavailable' = no authoritative surface exists yet (source_gap required); 'deferred' = surface exists but was intentionally not run this cycle (source_gap required).

reason.description: Why the signal is unavailable or deferred, naming the missing surface or the deferred command.

source.description: The authoritative surface that would compute this signal when it exists (script path, CI job, or artifact path).

follow_up.description: Optional next smallest step to make the signal available.

source.description: The authoritative surface used (script path, CI job, or artifact path). Required when availability is 'available'.

view.description: Whether the signals cover the full suite, only changed files, or a declared module subset.

baseline_ref.description: Git ref the report is compared against (e.g. 'origin/main'). Null when the report is absolute-only.

changed_files.description: Repository-root-relative changed files when view is 'changed_files'.

when.description: When the report is produced. Per-PR is advisory-only by default; mutation and long benchmarks stay scheduled/manual and are never forced per-PR.

lane_command.description: Canonical command that produces the report.

git_commit.description: Full Git commit hash the report was built from.

git_branch.description: Git branch name, or null in a detached HEAD state.

git_dirty.description: Whether uncommitted changes were present at report time. tree_clean is the negation of this value.

python_version.description: Python version string (major.minor.micro plus optional suffix).

platform.description: Platform identifier from platform.platform(), if captured.

key_packages.description: Package name to version mapping for packages that affect quality signals (e.g. pytest, coverage, mutmut, jsonschema).

command.description: The exact reproducible command that produced the report.

sources.description: Repository-root-relative authoritative source surfaces read (artifact paths or commands). Empty when all sources are live command output.

seed.description: Random seed if the producing command is stochastic, else null.

percentField.description: A percentage in the inclusive range 0..100.

role.description: Pass rate is a gate; collection completeness is a gate.

decision_use.description: How maintainers use this signal and which action it justifies.

pass_rate.description: Formula: passed / collected, where collected = passed + failed + errored (skips excluded from the denominator).

collection_completeness.description: Formula: collected / selected, where selected is the configured test selection. A value below 100% means some selected tests were not collected (collection error or deselection).

role.description: Line coverage absolute floor is a gate on non-PR CI; changed-file and branch coverage are diagnostics unless a lane enforces them.

branch.description: Formula: covered_branches / num_branches * 100. Reported as diagnostic when branch coverage is not enforced.

changed_files.description: Per-PR diagnostic: aggregate line coverage over the changed-file set vs the base ref.

min_threshold.description: Configured minimum changed-file coverage (default 80). Diagnostic unless a lane enforces it.

mutationSignal.description: Mutation score is always a scheduled diagnostic, never a per-PR gate. Categories are kept separately visible: killed, survived, equivalent, timeout, no_test, plus suspicious/skipped execution anomalies. No category is folded into another.

role.description: Diagnostic only. Never required per-PR before a baseline exists.

equivalent.description: Surviving mutants reviewed and judged semantically equivalent to the original. Reported separately from 'survived' so un-reviewed survivors are never hidden.

timeout.description: Mutants whose execution timed out; kept separate because they may mask undetected faults.

no_test.description: Generated mutants not exercised by any test; kept separate to expose coverage-of-assertions gaps.

mutation_score.description: Formula: killed / (total_mutants - equivalent) * 100, with equivalent excluded because they are not detectable faults. Reported as a diagnostic only.

equivalent_excluded.description: True when equivalent mutants are excluded from the denominator.

baselined_survivors.description: Survivors tolerated by the downward ratchet baseline; a new un-baselined survivor is the ratchet failure condition.

new_unbaselined_survivors.description: Suriving mutants not present in the committed baseline. Non-zero is a ratchet regression (diagnostic lane).

role.description: Duration is diagnostic; timeout-budget compliance can be a gate when a lane enforces per-test budgets.

timeout_budget_compliance.description: Per-test duration budget: count of tests exceeding the configured budget over the count measured.

budget_seconds.description: Configured per-test budget in seconds, or null when no budget is declared.

role.description: Flaky/rerun rate and skip/xfail age are diagnostics that inform quarantine and triage; they are not themselves merge gates.

flaky_rate.description: Formula: tests that failed at least once but passed on rerun / tests_run, over a rerun-enabled cycle.

rerun_rate.description: Formula: rerun_invocations / total_test_invocations. Measures retry volume.

skip_xfail_age.description: Age of skip/xfail markers since their introducing commit, to surface stale suppressions.

stale_threshold_days.description: Configured staleness threshold; entries older than this are flagged for re-triage.

contractScenarioCoverageSignal.description: Coverage of protected behavior contracts by tests: contract/schema, scenario, hazard/ODD, and compatibility dimensions. These are diagnostics that map critical behavior to its protecting test level; they are not yet enforced gates unless a lane declares so.

mapping_source.description: Artifact or script backing this dimension's covered/total counts, or null.

reproducibilitySignal.description: Reproducibility status of the deterministic lanes plus benchmark fallback/degraded counts. Fallback and degraded executions are kept separately visible and are never counted as success evidence (issue #6213 stop condition).

role.description: Strict-lane reproducibility is a gate for deterministic contracts; benchmark reproducibility is diagnostic.

strict_lane_reproducible.description: True when the deterministic strict lane reproduces for the same commit and environment. Null when not measured.

fallback_count.description: Benchmark runs that fell back to a degraded/native adapter path. Counted separately, never as success.

degraded_count.description: Benchmark runs that executed in a degraded mode. Counted separately, never as success.

native_count.description: Benchmark runs that executed on the intended native contract.

role.description: Cold/warm regression is advisory on PRs and enforced on main/dispatch; full performance smoke runs only in strict mode.

relative_change.description: (current - baseline) / baseline; positive is a slowdown.

enforced.description: True when this regression is enforced (fails the lane) in the current cadence.

escapedDefectsSignal.description: Defects that escaped the test suite and were found downstream (in production, release validation, or later cycles). A diagnostic leading indicator for test-strategy gaps; never aggregated into a vanity score.

escaped_count.description: Defects found downstream in the reporting window.

window.description: Reporting window for the escaped count (e.g. 'since last release').

linked_issues.description: Issue references for escaped defects captured this cycle.
